name: Riskfolio-Lib (Portfolio Optimization Library)

performance.notes: Convex optimization via cvxpy; performance scales with portfolio size

agent_readiness.notes: Portfolio optimization beyond mean-variance. import riskfolio as rp. port = rp.Portfolio(returns=returns). port.assets_stats(method_mu='hist', method_cov='hist'). w = port.optimization(model='Classic', rm='MV', obj='Sharpe'). Risk measures: MVaR, CVaR, CDaR, EVaR, WR, MDD. Black-Litterman. Risk parity. HRP (Hierarchical Risk Parity). Nested Cluster Optimization. Efficient frontier plotting. 20+ risk measures. Convex optimization via cvxpy.

gotchas: cvxpy dependency with solver backend (ECOS, OSQP, SCS) can be complex to install
20+ risk measures and models create steep learning curve
Optimization can fail with ill-conditioned covariance matrices
best_when: Advanced portfolio optimization beyond mean-variance (CVaR, CDaR, HRP)
Black-Litterman or risk parity portfolio construction
Hierarchical Risk Parity (HRP) for alternative diversification
avoid_when: When PyPortfolioOpt simpler API is sufficient
summary: Riskfolio-Lib provides advanced portfolio optimization: mean-variance, CVaR, CDaR, HRP, Black-Litterman, risk parity. port.optimization(model='Classic', rm='CVaR', obj='Sharpe'). 20+ risk measures via convex optimization (cvxpy). Efficient frontier, clustering-based allocation (HRP/HERC). More comprehensive than PyPortfolioOpt for research. Complex but powerful for institutional-grade portfolio construction.
